sanskrit:
असक्तबुद्धि: सर्वत्र जितात्मा विगतस्पृह: |
नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धिं परमां सन्न्यासेनाधिगच्छति ||
transliteration:
asakta-buddhiḥ sarvatra jitātmā vigata-spṛhaḥ |
aiṣkarmya-siddhiṁ paramāṁ sannyāsenādhigacchati ||
literal_meaning: One whose intelligence is unattached everywhere, who has conquered the mind and is free from craving, attains through renunciation the supreme perfection of action-less-ness.
contextual_meaning: True renunciation is an inner stance: non-attachment of thought, mastery over impulses, and absence of compensatory craving.  This psychological constellation automatically dissolves the sense of doership and its karmic residue, yielding a state of effortless right action.
psychological_state: Secure-autonomous attachment style: the mind is no longer hijacked by reward or rejection; actions flow from identified purpose rather than affect regulation.
trigger_scenario: You turn off notifications for a week, notice the itch to check the phone, breathe through it, and discover you can still perform excellently at work without the dopamine loops—realizing you don’t need the praise to keep going.
anchor_text: Let go inwardly; your actions will flow perfectly without residue.
